title: switchProjectCore uses process.cwd() (plugin dir) instead of user's real cwd
description: server.ts runs as an MCP child of claude with process.cwd() = plugin directory, not the user's project dir. switchProjectCore reads process.cwd() when building the handoff pointer and logging source path, which produces the wrong source alias/path every time. Fix: cli.ts writes CURRENT_CWD_FILE at launch; server.ts reads it via userCwd() helper.
notes: Bug was introduced in 0f65024 (first MCP switch_project impl), found in 7e7a458 E2E testing. The specific failing line is where it reads process.cwd() or passes it into handoff metadata; exact line depends on the intro commit state.

title: Config hot-reload watcher registered inside a conditional that never runs on cold start
description: The chrome.watch(CONFIG_FILE) call was inside an `if (existsSync(CONFIG_FILE))` guard. On cold start, the config file doesn't exist yet (daemon creates it), so the watcher is never registered. Subsequent edits to config.json don't trigger hot-reload. Fix: always register a watcher on the STATE_DIR (parent dir) so new config file creation + edits both fire.
notes: Subtle because the bug only manifests on fresh installs — dev machines that already had a config.json would never reproduce.

title: get_page_screenshot swallows inner error message, returns empty {} on failure
description: context-mcp's get_page_screenshot handler returns {success: request.status === 'completed', ...} but when status is 'failed', no top-level `error` field is set. The actual extension error (e.g. '非活动 tab 无法截图…') lives nested in request.error, which compass-llm's compressResultForDisplay drops. User/LLM sees an empty object and can't tell why the screenshot failed. Fix: surface request.error or a status-derived message to the top-level error field when success=false.
notes: Found via live E2E — user clicked Compass group tab that wasn't active, got {} back in chat UI, had to dig to find the real error.
